name: Mohenjo-daro
description: Mohenjo-daro, meaning 'Mound of the Dead', was one of the largest settlements of the Indus Valley Civilization. Located in modern-day Sindh, Pakistan, its ruins reveal remarkably sophisticated urban planning with a grid street layout, advanced drainage systems, and the Great Bath — one of the earliest known public water structures. The city may have housed up to 40,000 people at its peak.
